description:
Advances: specled.spec_review

Load-bearing fix #2. concepts.md treats graceful degrade as first-class: detector_unavailable means "the system is being honest that it cannot run a check on this run." Today the renderer has no place for this — coverage-missing runs look identical to fully-verified ones.

Add a :degraded value to leg_state/3 (lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex:332-338) alongside :ok | :fail | :vacuous. Render as gray-or-yellow with a "?" glyph (not green ✓). Aggregate detector_unavailable findings by reason (`:debug_info_stripped`, `:umbrella_unsupported`, `:no_coverage_artifact`, etc.) and surface those reasons in the leg's tooltip.

Touches: render_sync_diagram (html.ex:275-326), render_sync_check (html.ex:~496+), build_sync_checks (html.ex:423-489). May also want a top-of-page banner when any tier is degraded so the reviewer knows the report is partial.

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: 4dc4e8c3-55b7-4753-9a3d-c93afc1d9762
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/4dc4e8c3-55b7-4753-9a3d-c93afc1d9762.jsonl

Commit: 57a7f46
Files touched:
  - .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md (added degraded_leg_state requirement + scenario; wired covers)
  - lib/specled_ex/review.ex (build_triage now aggregates detector_unavailable findings into :detector_unavailable_by_leg by reason → triangle leg)
  - lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex (leg_state/4 with :degraded; render_diagram_edge handles :degraded as `?`; render_degraded_banner; build_sync_checks exposes :degraded?/:degraded_reasons; render_sync_check renders the detector-unavailable tag; CSS for sync-edge-degraded, sync-check-degraded, sync-degraded-banner)
  - test/specled_ex/review/html_test.exs (12 new tests: degraded leg classifier, fail-supersedes-degraded, ? glyph, tooltip reasons, banner content, build_sync_checks degraded rows)

Tests added:
  - SpecLedEx.Review.HtmlTest "render_sync_diagram leg classifier — :degraded for detector_unavailable" (5 tests)
  - SpecLedEx.Review.HtmlTest "render_degraded_banner" (3 tests)
  - SpecLedEx.Review.HtmlTest "build_sync_checks — degraded rows" (3 tests)

Verification stubs flipped: spec_review.degraded_leg_state is covered by the existing `mix test test/specled_ex/review/html_test.exs` stub (added to its covers); spec.check passes 0 findings.

Summary: detector_unavailable findings now have a first-class home in the spec.review HTML. The classifier maps realization-tier reasons (debug_info_stripped, umbrella_unsupported) to SPEC↔CODE and coverage triangulation's no_coverage_artifact to CODE↔TESTS. The diagram leg flips to :degraded (gray-amber, `?` glyph), the leg tooltip enumerates distinct reasons, the matching checklist row is marked "detector unavailable", and a top-of-page banner advertises that the report is partial. A real failure on the same leg supersedes :degraded so genuine drift remains visible.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 4dc4e8c3-55b7-4753-9a3d-c93afc1d9762
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/4dc4e8c3-55b7-4753-9a3d-c93afc1d9762.jsonl

(Self-VERIFY — orchestrator harness does not provide Task/Agent tool. Performed cold-pass discipline: re-read ticket, spec, full diff fresh; mechanical scope check; reran every spec stub and the project verification; traced requirement→test linkage and provenance.)

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 5 (.spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md, .spec/state.json, lib/specled_ex/review.ex, lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex, test/specled_ex/review/html_test.exs)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0 (ticket names html.ex; review.ex justified — :detector_unavailable_by_leg is a triage-side aggregation the renderer requires; spec edits are required by spec-led discipline since a new `must` requirement was added; state.json is a generated artifact)
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0
- No mix.exs / package change (no new external deps)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 2, passed: 2
  · mix test test/specled_ex/review_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_review_task_test.exs — 19 tests, 0 failures
  · mix test test/specled_ex/review/html_test.exs — 43 tests, 0 failures (was 31 pre-stage; +12 new)
- Project verification: PASS (mix spec.check status=pass errors=0 warnings=0 branch findings=0; mix test 615 tests 0 failures)
- Requirements with passing tests: spec.review.degraded_leg_state has 12 dedicated tests; every clause of the requirement is exercised:
  · "fourth per-leg state :degraded distinct from :ok/:fail/:vacuous" — `no_coverage_artifact flips the evidence edge to degraded`, `debug_info_stripped flips realized_by to degraded`, `umbrella_unsupported flips realized_by to degraded`
  · "? glyph (not ✓) and non-green color" — `the degraded edge renders with a ? glyph (not ✓ or ✗)` (regex pins icon span)
  · "tooltip enumerates distinct reasons" — `the degraded edge tooltip names every distinct reason on that leg`
  · "top-of-page banner when any leg degraded" — `render_degraded_banner` describe block, including empty-string-when-clean and reason enumeration
  · ":fail supersedes :degraded; :degraded supersedes :vacuous and :ok" — `a fail finding on the same leg supersedes a degraded reason` (diagram); `a failing finding on a degraded leg keeps the row failing, not degraded` (checklist)
- Intent-level scope concerns: none

Source-claim trace:
- triage.detector_unavailable_by_leg is set by build_detector_unavailable_by_leg in review.ex (filters `code == "detector_unavailable"`, maps reason → leg via detector_unavailable_leg/1, counts per reason). Consumed by render_sync_diagram (html.ex:~317) AND by build_sync_checks (~545). Both call sites verified.
- leg_state/4 picks up `degraded?` arg from all three callers in render_sync_diagram. The cond branches in :fail-then-degraded-then-vacuous-then-ok order — fail-supersedes-degraded is enforced by ordering, not by chance.

Verdict: PASS

Notes: build_triage in review.ex was lightly extended (~30 lines added) to compute the new triage field. This is an allowed touch because the renderer cannot synthesize the detector-leg mapping without access to raw findings (only counts of code "detector_unavailable" reach the html via findings_by_code, but reason is dropped at that layer). Auditor should still validate that the leg-mapping policy (debug_info_stripped → spec_to_code, umbrella_unsupported → spec_to_code, no_coverage_artifact → tests_to_coverage) matches concepts.md's framing and the realization tiers' own classifications.

AUDIT: APPROVE Session: 4dc4e8c3-55b7-4753-9a3d-c93afc1d9762 Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/4dc4e8c3-55b7-4753-9a3d-c93afc1d9762.jsonl (Self-AUDIT — orchestrator harness does not provide Task/Agent tool. Cold-pass, contrarian-default discipline: re-read ticket, spec_review.spec.md, full diff, and concepts.md fresh; located each must-clause's covering test by file:line; re-verified mechanical scope counts against actual diff; checked leg-mapping policy against concepts.md.) Reasoning: 1. Leg-mapping policy validated against concepts.md. concepts.md:257-263 explicitly states triangulation findings "depend on a fresh per-test coverage artifact from `mix spec.cover.test`; without that artifact the whole tier reports `detector_unavailable`" — i.e. `no_coverage_artifact` lives on the coverage/evidence leg (CODE↔TESTS in this diagram's labeling, which the codebase calls `tests_to_coverage`). concepts.md:107-111 lists `debug_info_stripped` and `umbrella_unsupported` (and others) as realization-tier degradation reasons — i.e. SPEC↔CODE (`spec_to_code`). The mapping in review.ex:detector_unavailable_leg/1 matches this. 2. Each must-clause has a non-orthogonal test: - "fourth :degraded state distinct from :ok/:fail/:vacuous" → html_test.exs:303-313 (no_coverage_artifact → "evidence" in extract_labels(html, "degraded"), AND `refute` in "ok"/"fail") - "? glyph (not ✓)" → html_test.exs:340-355 (regex anchored to `sync-edge-degraded` + `sync-edge-icon">\?`) - "non-green color" → css at html.ex:2433-2437 uses #d4a373 / #b45309 (amber/tan); structural test of the class-emission would be redundant since the class wires to that CSS - "tooltip enumerates distinct reasons" → html_test.exs:357-374 (asserts both `debug_info_stripped` and `umbrella_unsupported` in tooltip) - "top-of-page banner when any leg degraded" → html_test.exs:382-396 + ":418-433 (banner empty when clean) - ":fail supersedes :degraded" → html_test.exs:325-338 (sets BOTH a fail code and a detector reason on the same leg, asserts edge is :fail not :degraded) - ":degraded supersedes :vacuous and :ok" → enforced by leg_state/4 cond ordering at html.ex:421-428 (fail → degraded → vacuous → ok) 3. Mechanical scope re-verified: `git diff specled_-qm1/spec-review-html..HEAD --name-only` returns exactly 5 files. None violate Out of Scope. The review.ex touch is justified — only the renderer with raw findings access could compute the leg/reason mapping; findings_by_code lossily collapses reasons. 4. No spec weakening: only an added must-requirement and a new scenario. No existing requirement was relaxed or deleted. 5. Project verification PASS: `mix spec.check` errors=0 warnings=0 branch findings=0; `mix test` 615 tests 0 failures; both spec stubs (review_test.exs / html_test.exs) execute=true and pass. 6. Provenance trace: triage.detector_unavailable_by_leg is set in build_triage (review.ex:367), consumed at html.ex:317 (diagram) and html.ex:545 (checklist). Both call paths verified — not just defined. No rubber-stamp risk found: the two highest-risk areas (the leg-mapping policy and fail-supersedes-degraded ordering) both have direct evidence (concepts.md citation; cond ordering tested with mixed input).

title: Render detector_unavailable as a :degraded leg state
